GitHub

AI Gateway · MCP control · Admin API

One control layer for AI traffic, keys and agents.

Nexus API Balancer gives AI tools one clean entry point and handles the hard parts behind it: model routing, provider selection, key rotation, limits, client access, MCP control and admin endpoints.

Open repository Explain it simply
01 Single endpoint
02 MCP control layer
03 Admin API endpoints
Plain English summary

A smart traffic controller for AI tools.

Nexus is like a secure reception desk for AI services. Instead of giving every app separate access to every provider, you connect tools to Nexus once. Nexus then decides which model provider should handle the request and keeps keys, limits and clients under control.

For HR This project demonstrates backend architecture, infrastructure thinking, API design, security awareness and product-level presentation.
For teams One gateway can simplify experiments with OpenAI, Gemini, Groq, Mistral, Anthropic-style APIs and local AI-agent tooling.
For developers It offers OpenAI-compatible routing, dynamic model discovery, API key pooling, client isolation, MCP access and Scalar docs.
Capabilities

Compact enough for a recruiter. Concrete enough for an engineer.

Each capability is written in plain language first, with enough signal to show that the project is real infrastructure rather than a cosmetic wrapper.

One gateway endpoint

Connect AI clients to one stable address instead of configuring every provider separately.

Dynamic model routing

Nexus can discover provider models and route requests to the correct pool automatically.

Key orchestration

Multiple API keys can be loaded, separated and rotated without exposing secrets to every tool.

Limit-aware control

The gateway can respect request and token limits, helping traffic stay predictable.

Client isolation

Different users or tools can be assigned different access rules and provider pools.

MCP-ready workflows

Includes a JSON-RPC MCP layer for agent-driven management of pools, keys and configuration workflows.

MCP control layer

Not just a proxy — it can be managed by AI agents.

Nexus exposes an MCP-compatible JSON-RPC endpoint so agent tools can inspect pools, read safe config data and perform controlled management actions.

HTTP MCP endpoint

POST /mcp acts as the programmatic control surface for AI-agent workflows.

stdio bridge

The nexus_balancer mcp mode can bridge local MCP clients to the HTTP server.

Pool & key operations

Agents can list pools and, with admin rights, update descriptions or import/export keys.

Run MCP stdio bridge 
NEXUS_MCP_URL="http://localhost:3317/mcp" \
NEXUS_API_KEY="<token>" \
nexus_balancer mcp
Admin API today · UI panel planned

Control endpoints exist. A visual admin panel is the next step.

The current project includes admin routes for statistics, configuration, client registration and key management. There is no finished web dashboard in the repository yet, so the landing page presents this honestly as an Admin API with a planned UI panel.

GET /stats GET /config PATCH /config POST /admin/clients GET /admin/keys/{pool}/{key_id} POST /admin/keys/{pool}
Request flow

What happens after a tool calls Nexus?

01 Client calls one endpoint

Cline, Roo, OpenCode, LM Studio or another tool sends an OpenAI-compatible request.

02 Access is checked

Nexus validates the client before provider keys are used.

03 Model is resolved

The requested model is matched to an available provider pool.

04 Key and limits are selected

The gateway chooses an available key and applies configured safety limits.

05 Request is proxied

The provider handles the work while the client still sees one clean API surface.

06 Control remains centralized

MCP and Admin API endpoints keep pool, key, config and client management in one place.

Docker quick start

Run it as a containerized gateway.

The repository includes a Dockerfile and docker-compose setup for mounting config, env, data and secrets into the runtime container.

Prepare files 
cp .env.example .env
cp config.yaml.example config.yaml
mkdir -p secrets data
Start with compose 
docker compose up --build
# Gateway: http://localhost:3317
# Scalar:  http://localhost:3317/scalar
Mounted paths 
./config.yaml → /app/config.yaml
./.env        → /app/.env
./data        → /app/data
./secrets     → /app/secrets
